data.title: Japan's Hotter-Than-Expected Core Inflation Boosts Odds of BOJ Rate Hike in July — Market Talk
data.description: Japan's hotter-than-expected core inflation boosts odds of a Bank of Japan rate hike in July, ING's Min Joo Kang says in a research report. Consumer prices excluding fresh food rose 3.5% in April from a year earlier, up from March's 3.2% increase, and exceeded market consensus of a 3.4% increase, the senior economist for South Korea and Japan notes. Excluding fresh food and energy, core-core CPI rose 3.0% on year in April, which suggests underlying inflation will remain above the BOJ's target of 2.0%, the economist says. After a potential 25bp rate hike in July, the BOJ may stand pat for a considerable time owing to high uncertainty surrounding U.S. tariffs, the economist adds. (ronnie.harui@wsj.com)

data.title: ProCap Acquisition Corp: Offering Priced at $10 Per Unit, Resulting in Gross Proceeds of $250M >PCAPU
ProCap Acquisition Corp Closes Upsized IPO of 25M Units >PCAPU
CIMC Group: To Repurchase Up to HK$500M Worth of H-Shares
Ascent Bridge Forecasts Net Loss for Fiscal 2025
data.description:
Ascent Bridge expects to book a net loss for the fiscal year ended March 31, according to a filing with the Singapore Exchange on Thursday.

The company attributed this to a decrease in revenue due to reduced contributions from its duty-paid markets.

data.title: Livzon Pharmaceutical Group to Acquire 64.8% Stake in Vietnam's Imexpharm for 1.6 Billion Yuan
data.description:
Livzon Pharmaceutical Group agreed to buy a 64.81% stake in Vietnam-listed Imexpharm (HOSE:IMP), which specializes in antibiotics and cardiovascular drugs, for about 1.59 billion yuan.

The stake will be purchased from SK Investment Vina III, Sunrise Kim Investment and KBA Investment, according to a Friday filing with the Shenzhen bourse.

The deal, slated to close within nine months, awaits regulatory clearances in Vietnam.

data.title: Indian benchmarks may open higher; foreign flows, US fiscal woes seen capping gains
data.description:
India's benchmark indexes are likely to open higher on Friday, after logging a decline of 1.65% so far this week, although lingering concerns over foreign inflows and the United States' worsening fiscal health could limit gains.

Gift Nifty futures (GIFc1) were trading at 24,692.5 as of 7:59 a.m. IST, indicating that the Nifty 50 will open above its previous close of 24,609.7.

Foreign portfolio investors (FPIs) sold Indian shares worth 50.45 billion rupees ($586.8 million) on Thursday, as per provisional data, the third such session of selling in four days.

Thirty-year U.S. bond yields reached their highest level in 19 months before easing on Thursday, after the U.S. House of Representatives passed President Donald Trump's tax bill, which is expected to add about $3.8 trillion to the federal government's debt pile over the next decade.

Concerns over mounting debt and Moody's downgrade of the U.S. credit rating last week have weighed on stocks globally in the last few sessions.

Rising Treasury yields tend to make bonds more attractive to foreign investors, driving out capital from stocks in emerging markets such as India.

Asian markets were cautious on the day, with MSCI Asia ex Japan (.MIAPJ0000PUS) up 0.1%, after a 0.9% drop in the previous session.

The short-term trend in India remains weak and any rise towards the 24,800 level on Nifty could attract selling pressure, said Rupak De, senior technical analyst at LKP Securities.

STOCKS TO WATCH

** Fashion retailer Trent and state-owned defence firm Bharat Electronics to replace IndusInd Bank and Nestle India in BSE Sensex from June 23

** ITC posts higher quarterly profit on the back of strength in its mainstay cigarettes business and resilient rural demand

** Sun Pharmaceutical Industries reports bigger adjusted quarterly profit, driven by strong domestic demand for its drugs to treat rare diseases

data.title: China Stocks Gain on Trade Optimism
data.description:
The Shanghai Composite rose 0.1% to around 3,384 while the Shenzhen Component gained 0.5% to 10,266 on Friday, recouping losses from the previous session as sentiment around Sino-US trade negotiations improved.

China’s Foreign Ministry announced that Washington and Beijing have agreed to keep communication channels open, following a call between Chinese Vice Foreign Minister Ma Zhaoxu and US Deputy Secretary of State Kurt Campbell.

The move has eased investor concerns over escalating tensions and bolstered hopes for renewed dialogue.

Meanwhile, the People’s Bank of China injected 500 billion yuan into the banking system via its one-year medium-term lending facility, aiming to maintain ample liquidity and support credit growth amid a fragile economic recovery.

High-growth sectors led the rally, with notable gains in technology and electric vehicle stocks.

Hydsoft Technology surged 4.6%, Kyland Technology jumped 9.0%, Seres Group advanced 5.6%, and BYD Company added 2.7%.

data.title: South Korean shares flat on US policy, bond market caution
data.description:
Key points:
  • KOSPI rises, foreigners net sellers
  • Korean won strengthens against dollar
  • South Korea benchmark bond yield rises

Round-up of South Korean financial markets:

** South Korean shares were flat on Friday, weighed by caution over U.S. President Donald Trump's tax-cut bill and remarks from a South Korean minister that officials are monitoring the domestic impact of U.S. bond market volatility.

** The benchmark KOSPI held its ground at 2,593.97, as of 0214 GMT.

** U.S. stocks closed a choppy session little changed on Thursday, erasing initial declines as Treasury yields eased off recent highs after the House of Representatives passed Trump's tax and spending bill.

** South Korea pledged on Wednesday more support measures for key export industries such as biopharmaceuticals and autos, as Trump's sweeping tariffs weigh on the trade-reliant economy.

** Acting Finance Minister Kim Beom-seok said on Friday that authorities plan to closely monitor domestic market impact from heightened volatility in the Unites States and Japan bond markets.

** Among index heavyweights, South Korean chipmaker Samsung Electronics fell 0.18%, while peer SK Hynix gained 1.22%. Battery maker LG Energy Solution slid 2.37%.

** Shares of Hyundai Motor and sister automaker Kia Corp were down 1.43% and 1.79%, respectively. Steelmaker POSCO Holdings shed 1.68% and drugmaker Samsung BioLogics fell 3.15%.

** Of the total 937 traded issues, 510 shares advanced, while 387 declined.

** Foreigners were net sellers of shares worth 39.6 billion won ($28.77 million).

** The won was quoted at 1,376.8 per dollar on the onshore settlement platform , 0.43% higher than its previous close at 1,382.7.

** In money and debt markets, June futures on three-year treasury bonds (KTBc1) lost 0.03 point to 107.55.

** The most liquid three-year Korean treasury bond yield (KR3YT=RR) was flat at 2.336%, while the benchmark 10-year yield rose by 2.2 basis points to 2.766%.

($1 = 1,376.6000 won)

data.title: HDB Financial Services to issue multiple tenor bonds, bankers say
data.description:
India's HDB Financial Services plans to raise 16 billion rupees ($186.10 million) through the sale of bonds maturing in three years and in five years, three bankers said on Friday.

The non-banking finance company has invited bids for both options later in the day, they said.

HDB Financial Services did not respond to a Reuters email seeking comment.

data.title: Indonesia appoints former military officer as head of customs and excise
data.description:
Indonesian Finance Minister Sri Mulyani Indrawati on Friday appointed retired Lieutenant General Djaka Budhi Utama, a former military officer who was once found guilty of kidnapping rights activists, as the head of the country's customs and excise unit.

Sri Mulyani also appointed Bimo Wijayanto, a civil servant who was previously posted to the tax office, investment ministry and president's chief of staff, as the new head of the tax office.

data.title: Columbia Violated Students' Civil Rights, Government Investigation Finds — WSJ
data.description:
By Liz Essley Whyte and Douglas Belkin

A government investigation found that Columbia University violated its students' rights, the Department of Health and Human Services Office for Civil Rights said Thursday.

Columbia has been locked in a battle with the Trump administration over antisemitism concerns since March when the government canceled $400 million in federal funds.

This is a developing story and will be updated.

Write to Liz Essley Whyte at liz.whyte@wsj.com and Douglas Belkin at Doug.Belkin@wsj.com

data.title: NZ Dollar Gains on Strong Retail Sales Data
data.description:
The New Zealand dollar rose to around $0.591 on Friday following stronger-than-expected retail sales data.

Retail sales grew by 0.8% over the quarter in the first quarter of 2025, beating expectations of a 0.1% rise.

On Thursday, the currency dropped 0.7% as the government's tight budget position signaled more policy easing.

New Zealand’s government has revised its fiscal outlook, projecting a smaller budget deficit of NZ$14.74 billion for the fiscal year ending June 2025, emphasizing fiscal prudence even as it warned of softer GDP growth and a "trade shock" to the economy amid the global trade war.

The Reserve Bank of New Zealand is widely expected to cut rates by another 25bps to 3.25% next week.

So far this week, the Kiwi dollar has gained 0.5%, marking its first rise in four weeks.

data.title: Japan futures tick up, set for weekly rise on Thai weather warning
data.description:
• Japanese rubber futures ticked up on Friday and were set for a weekly rise on worsening weather in top producer Thailand, though gains were capped by the upcoming harvesting season and softer oil prices.

  • The Osaka Exchange (OSE) rubber contract for October delivery , rose 1.1 yen, or 0.34%, to 323 yen ($2.25) per kg as of 0215 GMT.

  • The contract has climbed 3.1% so far this week.

  • The rubber contract on the Shanghai Futures Exchange (SHFE) for September delivery fell 120 yuan, or 0.81%, to 14,765 yuan ($2,050.47) per metric ton.

  • Thailand's meteorological agency warned farmers of possible crop damage, adding that heavy rains and accumulations may cause flash floods from May 23-27.

  • The current raw material output is temporarily low as May and June mark transition periods for main production areas to start harvesting, said China International Capital Corp (CICC) Futures.

  • Rubber crops usually undergo a season of low production from February to May, before a peak harvesting period that lasts until September.

  • Expectations of increased production during the peak season are stoking fears of oversupply, CICC said.

  • The most active June butadiene rubber contract on the SHFE (SHBRv1) lost 190 yuan, or 1.57%, to 11,925 yuan ($1,656.07) per metric ton, pressuring prices since natural rubber competes with synthetic rubber.

  • Oil slipped on the day, weighed down by a stronger U.S. dollar and the possibility that OPEC+ will further increase its crude oil output.

  • Natural rubber often takes direction from oil prices as synthetic rubber is made from crude oil.

  • The front-month rubber contract on the Singapore Exchange's SICOM platform for June delivery last traded at 171.5 U.S. cents per kg, up 0.1%.

($1 = 143.6000 yen)

($1 = 7.2008 Chinese yuan)

data.title: British Business - May 23
data.description:
The following are the top stories on the business pages of British newspapers. Reuters has not verified these stories and does not vouch for their accuracy.

The Times

- Artificial intelligence lab Anthropic's new head of its business in Europe, the Middle East and Africa, Guillaume Princen, said the AI company was seeing "huge demand" in Britain.

- Rio Tinto CEO Jakob Stausholm, who in his 4-1/2-year tenure oversaw a big bet on lithium and a cultural overhaul at a firm strongly criticised for workplace toxicity, is to step down, the miner said in a surprise to investors.

The Guardian

- British Minister for Public Health Ashley Dalton said the government intends to make and lay a statutory instrument (SI) to explicitly exempt 'brand advertising' from the restrictions.

- Britain's House of Commons justice committee accused the leader of the Criminal Cases Review Commission (CCRC) Karen Kneller of giving "problematic" evidence to the committee and said her position was no longer tenable.

The Telegraph

- Tesco , Britain's biggest supermarket group, said Matthew Barnes, its British boss who was seen by some analysts as the likely successor to group CEO Ken Murphy, has stepped down after just 15 months in the job.

- British chemicals company Johnson Matthey has agreed to sell its catalyst technologies business to Honeywell International for 1.8 billion pounds ($2.42 billion), including debt.

Sky News

- Phoenix Group , the FTSE-100 pensions provider, is planning to rebrand itself using the historic Standard Life name it acquired four years ago.

- Britain signed a deal to cede sovereignty of the Chagos Islands to Mauritius after a London judge overturned a last-minute injunction and cleared the way for an agreement the government says is vital to protect the nation's security.

($1 = 0.7448 pounds)

data.title: Auric Mining Raises Nearly AU$6.7 Million via Share Placement; Shares Up 11%
data.description:
Auric Mining received firm commitments to raise AU$6.7 million through the issue of 37 million shares at AU$0.18 apiece, according to a Friday filing with the Australian bourse.

Proceeds will be used for the purchase of the Burbanks facility and the acquisition of the Lindsay's project, including the Parrot Feathers gold mine, in Western Australia, the filing said.

QA Capital was the lead manager and sole bookrunner for the placement.

Shares rose 11% in midday trade Friday.

data.title: Cordlife Reports Material Variances in 2024 Figures
data.description:
Cordlife reported material variances in its figures for the year ended Dec. 31, 2024, according to a filing with the Singapore Exchange on Thursday.

The company's revenue declined by SG$$197,000 or 0.7% to SG$27.6 million, due to additional provision made for the affected Tank A and High-Ris Tanks.

The change also impacted loss per share for the period.

Shares of the company were up nearly 2% in recent trading.

data.title: Hong Kong Shares Eye Longest Winning Streak Since February
data.description:
Hong Kong stocks rose 89 points or 0.4% to 23,627 in early trade Friday, swinging from the previous session’s drop after the U.S. and China agreed to maintain open communication following a call between Chinese Vice Foreign Minister Ma Zhaoxu and U.S. Deputy Secretary of State Christopher Landau.

Sentiment was also lifted by the PBoC’s recent move to cut key interest rates, including those at major state-owned banks, in a step expected to prompt smaller lenders to follow suit amid shrinking net interest margins.

Gains were broad-based, led by financials, technology, and consumer stocks.

For the week, the Hang Seng is on track to post its sixth consecutive gain—its longest winning streak since February—rising around 1.0% so far, on upbeat earnings.

However, upside momentum was capped by concerns over U.S. fiscal stability after the House passed a costly tax bill that unsettled markets.

Among notable gainers were Smoore Holdings (4.0%), Li Auto (2.0%), and KE Holdings (1.9%).

data.title: Rio Tinto's Next CEO May Come From Within — Market Talk
data.description: The market will likely expect the successor for Rio Tinto CEO Jakob Stausholm to be an internal candidate, according to Citi analyst Paul McTaggart. He views iron-ore chief Simon Trott and Chief Commercial Officer Bold Baatar, a former copper boss, as the likely front runners. Like other analysts, McTaggart says the succession plan came as a surprise. "We won't speculate as to why Stausholm is leaving earlier than most would have expected," he says in a note. Citi has a neutral rating and A$130.00 target on Rio Tinto, which is down 1.0% at A$117.64.(rhiannon.hoyle@wsj.com; @RhiannonHoyle)

data.title: Eco-Shop Shares Jump on Debut, Signaling IPO Market Strength in Malaysia
data.description:
(01:04 GMT) *Eco-Shop Shares Open at MYR1.25 on First Day of Trading
(01:09 GMT) *Eco-Shop Priced Its IPO at MYR1.13/Share
(02:33 GMT) Eco-Shop Shares Jump on Debut, Signaling IPO Market Strength in Malaysia

By Ying Xian Wong

Shares of Malaysian dollar-store chain Eco-Shop soared in their first day of trading, a debut that could encourage other companies to tap the country's initial public offering market.

The stock opened at 1.25 ringgit, equivalent to $0.29, on the benchmark KLCI index on Friday, an 11% increase over the IPO price.

Eco-Shop, which sells household merchandise at a fixed price of under one dollar, raised about $974 million through its IPO--the largest listing in Malaysia so far this year.

The listing is likely to boost appetite for listings among Malaysian companies, such as bubble-tea chain Tealive and private healthcare group Sunway Healthcare Group. Dow Jones Newswires has previously reported on the companies' IPO plans.

Global equities markets are showing signs of stabilizing from the spike in volatility over the past months, steadying on the de-escalation of tensions between the U.S. and major trading partners, including China.

Malaysia led Southeast Asia in IPO fundraising last year, raising a total of $1.7 billion. According to the local stock exchange, 55 companies went public on Bursa Malaysia in 2024, marking the highest number of listings in 19 years.

So far this year, 23 companies have gone public on Bursa Malaysia.

Founded in 2003, Eco-Shop operates 358 stores across Malaysia. It commands a significant share of the retail segment, accounting for nearly 68% of the dollar store space by revenue last year, and aims to open 70 new stores annually over the next five years.

Write to Ying Xian Wong at yingxian.wong@wsj.com

data.title: Iran's Foreign Minister lays down conditions for a deal with the US ahead of weekend talks
data.description:
Iran’s Foreign Minister announced via social media that he is heading to Rome for a fifth round of indirect talks with the United States, underscoring the urgency and clarity of Tehran’s negotiating stance.

In the message, he framed the path to a potential agreement in blunt terms:

“Zero nuclear weapons = we DO have a deal.

Zero enrichment = we do NOT have a deal.”

The remarks suggest that while Iran remains open to diplomacy, it is unwilling to forgo uranium enrichment—long a sticking point in nuclear negotiations. The talks come amid renewed efforts to revive diplomacy and break the deadlock over Iran’s nuclear program.

The outcome of the talks should influence oil early in the new week. Its probably likely that the deadlock will persist. We'll see.

data.title: Jiemei Electronic and Technology Signs Cooperation Deal with Battery Maker
data.description:
Zhejiang Jiemei Electronic and Technology subsidiary Rouzhen Technology signed a strategic cooperation framework agreement with a solid-state battery manufacturer.

Under the agreement, the two parties will jointly develop high-safety, lightweight composite current collectors, with Rouzhen Technology to supply these components based on the battery firm's demand, according to a Thursday filing with the Shenzhen bourse.

In 2025 alone, Rouzhen Technology will reserve a production capacity of 600,000 square meters for the battery manufacturer, who will purchase at least 70% of that capacity.

Shares of the electronic components manufacturer were down nearly 2% in recent trade.

data.title: Fortescue slips in line with mining index; Bell Potter cuts PT on Iron Bridge delay
data.description:
** Shares of Australian miner Fortescue fall 1.1% to A$15.71

data.title: H2G Green Flags Higher Net Loss for Fiscal H2
data.description:
H2G Green is expecting to book a higher net loss for the fiscal half ended march 31, compared with the previous half, according to a filing with the Singapore Exchange on Thursday.

The comp any has attributed this to impairment of non-current assets and higher depreciation charges.

The company will release its financial results on or before May 30.

data.title: Deals done by a producer from Taiwan for Ethylene-vinyl acetate (EVA) in Bangladesh
data.description: A Polymerupdate source in Bangladesh reported that a Taiwanese producer is heard to have sealed deals for EVA (VA 18%) grade at the USD 1270/mt levels. This deals is for June 2025 shipment on CFR Chattogram port basis.

data.title: Celanese keeps etac/butac plant off stream

data.title: Frencken Group May Gain From Semiconductor Segment — Market Talk
data.description: Frencken Group's semiconductor segment is likely to be its main growth driver, Maybank Securities analyst Jarick Seet writes in a note. The technology solutions provider's management expects its semiconductor sales to increase in 1H, especially in Asia, Seet says. Frencken's 1Q profit after tax and minority interests rose 12% on year to S$10.0 million, driven by a 34% on year increase in its semiconductor segment's revenue, Seet notes. Maybank likes the stock and believes it will remain a key beneficiary of the semiconductor industry recovery. It maintains a buy rating and target price of S$1.34 on the stock. Shares are flat at S$1.14.(amanda.lee@wsj.com)

data.title: Great Boulder Resources Says Drilling at Western Australia Gold Project Confirms Additional Gold Mineralization, Shares Climb 3%
data.description:
Great Boulder Resources said reverse circulation drilling at its Side Well gold project in Western Australia, beneath recent gold discoveries, confirmed additional gold mineralization, according to a Friday Australian bourse filing.

Significant intercepts encountered during the drilling included 14 meters at 1.64 grams per tonne grade of gold from 83 meters, as well as 19 meters at 0.84 g/t grade of gold from 28 meters.

The firm is awaiting assays from 16 reverse circulation drill holes.

Meanwhile, air core drilling extended the Eaglehawk deposit by a further 200 meters, with a "significant" intercept of eight meters at 2.19 g/t grade of gold from 60 meters.

Its shares climbed 3% in recent trading on Friday.

data.title: Toyota cuts stake in Aichi Steel to 20.75% from 23.71%, regulatory filing shows
data.description:
Aichi Steel Corp :

  • TOYOTA CUTS STAKE IN AICHI STEEL TO 20.75% FROM 23.71%, REGULATORY FILING SHOWS

data.title: United Overseas Bank Lifts 2025 Singapore Economy Growth Forecast to 1.7%
data.description:
United Overseas Bank's Global Economics & Market Research division raised its 2025 GDP growth forecast for Singapore to 1.7%, according to a research report released on Thursday.

Earlier, the division had estimated 2025 economic growth for the city-state at 1.5%.

The lender said growth momentum in the second quarter of the year could continue, mainly driven by the current pause on reciprocal tariffs following a US-China truce.

However, the 2026 GDP growth forecast has been lowered to 1.4% after a previous forecast of 1.6%.
